title: L2: Formulas & Cell References
description: Build, replicate and understand spreadsheet formulas (Income = Quantity x Price) ready for BTEC DIT Component 2 Task 2a.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Quick recap from Lesson 1. Type short answers.
steps.js:
(function(){var qs=[{q:'What is the name for one single box in a spreadsheet?',a:['cell']},{q:'Rows are labelled with numbers. What are columns labelled with?',a:['letters','a letter']},{q:'Write the cell reference for column B, row 4.',a:['b4']},{q:'What do we call data that has been given meaning or context?',a:['information']}];var c=document.getElementById('l2-dna');c.innerHTML=qs.map(function(x,i){return '';}).join('')+'
';document.getElementById('l2-dna-c').onclick=function(){var ok=0;qs.forEach(function(x,i){var el=document.getElementById('l2-dna-'+i);var v=el.value.trim().toLowerCase();var pass=x.a.some(function(a){return v===a;});el.classList.toggle('correct',pass);el.classList.toggle('wrong',!pass);if(pass)ok++;});var fb=document.getElementById('l2-dna-fb');if(ok===qs.length){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Great recall. Now let us learn formulas.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='You have '+ok+' / '+qs.length+'. Fix the red boxes and check again.';}};})();

steps.title: What is a Formula?
steps.content:
What is a formula?

A formula tells the spreadsheet to do a calculation for you. Every formula starts with an equals sign =.

Maths operators in spreadsheets

You want to...SymbolExampleAnswer
Add+=5+38
Subtract-=10-46
Multiply*=6*212
Divide/=20/45

Your turn. Click cell B2 in the sheet below, type =5+3 and press Enter. The cell shows the answer, not the formula. Then click Check.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js',function(){var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l2-try-sheet'),{data:[['','',''],['','',''],['','','']],columns:[{width:110},{width:110},{width:110}],minDimensions:[3,3],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l2-try-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l2-try-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l2-try-fb');var grid=sheet.getData();var found=false;for(var y=0;y<grid.length;y++){for(var x=0;x<grid[y].length;x++){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(x,y,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(grid[y][x]);if(raw.charAt(0)==='='&&Math.abs(val-8)<0.001){found=true;}}}if(found){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Yes! =5+3 calculates to 8. Notice the cell shows 8, and the formula bar shows the formula.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Type =5+3 into a cell (start with the = sign) and press Enter, then Check.';}};});});});})();

steps.title: Writing Simple Formulas
steps.content:
Writing simple formulas - practice

Write each formula. Remember to start with =. Spaces do not matter.
steps.js:
(function(){var qs=[{q:'Write a formula that adds 4 and 6.',p:'^=\\s*(4\\s*\\+\\s*6|6\\s*\\+\\s*4)\\s*$',hint:'=4+6'},{q:'Write a formula that multiplies 5 by 3.',p:'^=\\s*(5\\s*\\*\\s*3|3\\s*\\*\\s*5)\\s*$',hint:'=5*3'},{q:'Write a formula that subtracts 2 from 10.',p:'^=\\s*10\\s*-\\s*2\\s*$',hint:'=10-2'},{q:'Write a formula that divides 20 by 4.',p:'^=\\s*20\\s*/\\s*4\\s*$',hint:'=20/4'}];var c=document.getElementById('l2-ops');c.innerHTML=qs.map(function(x,i){return '';}).join('')+'
';document.getElementById('l2-ops-c').onclick=function(){var ok=0;qs.forEach(function(x,i){var el=document.getElementById('l2-ops-'+i);var pass=new RegExp(x.p).test(el.value.trim());el.classList.toggle('correct',pass);el.classList.toggle('wrong',!pass);if(pass)ok++;});var fb=document.getElementById('l2-ops-fb');if(ok===qs.length){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='All correct. You can build any calculation now.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='You have '+ok+' / '+qs.length+'. Start with = and use the right symbol (+ - * /).';}};})();

steps.title: Using Cell References
steps.content:
Using cell references

Instead of putting numbers in a formula, we point at the cells that hold them. =A2*B2 means “multiply whatever is in A2 by whatever is in B2”.

This is powerful: if the numbers change, the answer updates automatically. This is exactly what BTEC Task 2 needs.

In the sheet below, C2 already contains =A2*B2 and shows 6 (because 2 × 3).

Try it: change the Quantity in B2 to 10 and press Enter. Watch the Total change by itself, then click Check.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js',function(){var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l2-ref-sheet'),{data:[['Price (£)','Quantity','Total (£)'],[2,3,'=A2*B2']],columns:[{width:100},{width:100},{width:110}],minDimensions:[3,2],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l2-ref-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l2-ref-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l2-ref-fb');var grid=sheet.getData();var total=parseFloat(grid[1][2]);if(Math.abs(total-20)<0.001){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Exactly. You did not retype the answer - the formula recalculated for you. That is why we use cell references.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Set B2 to 10 so the Total becomes 20, then Check. (The formula =A2*B2 does the rest.)';}};});});});})();

steps.title: Worked Example: Income
steps.content:
Worked example: Income from sales

BTEC Task 2a asks you to calculate Income from sales for each item. Click through how it is built.

=B2*C2

steps.title: Income Practice (Task 2a)
steps.content:
Your turn: calculate the Income

Fill in the Income column (column D) for every item using a formula. For Pens, click D2 and type =B2*C2, then Enter. Do the same for each row.

Tip: the formula bar at the top shows the formula in the selected cell.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js',function(){var data=[['Item','Quantity','Price (£)','Income (£)'],['Pens',12,0.5,''],['Notebooks',8,1.2,''],['Erasers',20,0.3,''],['Folders',5,1.5,'']];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l2-income-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:120},{width:90},{width:90},{width:110}],minDimensions:[4,5],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l2-income-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l2-income-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l2-income-fb');var grid=sheet.getData();var rows=[[1,12,0.5],[2,8,1.2],[3,20,0.3],[4,5,1.5]];var usedFormula=true,allOk=true;for(var i=0;i<rows.length;i++){var y=rows[i][0];var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,y,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(grid[y][3]);var expect=rows[i][1]*rows[i][2];if(raw.charAt(0)!=='=')usedFormula=false;if(isNaN(val)||Math.abs(val-expect)>0.005)allOk=false;}if(!usedFormula){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Use a FORMULA in every Income cell (start with =). For Pens type =B2*C2, for Notebooks =B3*C3, and so on.';return;}if(!allOk){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Some Income values are not right yet. Income = Quantity × Price for that row.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Brilliant - every Income is worked out with a formula. That is exactly the skill marked in Task 2a.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Replicating a Formula
steps.content:
Replicating (copying) a formula

BTEC says: “Use a formula for the first item, then replicate it for all other items.” Replicate just means copy the formula down - you do not type it again every time.

The clever part: when you copy =B2*C2 down one row, it automatically becomes =B3*C3. The references move with it.

How to replicate

1. Click the cell that already has the formula (D2).
2. Copy it: Ctrl + C.
3. Select the empty cells below (D3 to D5).
4. Paste: Ctrl + V.

Try it: D2 already has =B2*C2. Replicate it down so every row has an Income, then click Check.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js',function(){var data=[['Item','Quantity','Price (£)','Income (£)'],['Rulers',15,0.4,'=B2*C2'],['Markers',6,1.1,''],['Glue sticks',10,0.8,''],['Scissors',4,2.5,'']];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l2-rep-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:120},{width:90},{width:90},{width:110}],minDimensions:[4,5],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l2-rep-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l2-rep-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l2-rep-fb');var grid=sheet.getData();var rows=[[2,6,1.1],[3,10,0.8],[4,4,2.5]];var usedFormula=true,allOk=true;for(var i=0;i<rows.length;i++){var y=rows[i][0];var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,y,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(grid[y][3]);var expect=rows[i][1]*rows[i][2];if(raw.charAt(0)!=='=')usedFormula=false;if(isNaN(val)||Math.abs(val-expect)>0.005)allOk=false;}if(!usedFormula){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Copy the formula from D2 and paste it into D3, D4 and D5 (do not type the answers).';return;}if(!allOk){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite - make sure you replicated D2 into every row below.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Replicated perfectly. Notice each row used =B?*C? for its own row - the references adjusted automatically.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of lesson exam

Answer every question. Feedback appears after each one.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l2-exam',[{q:'Every spreadsheet formula must start with which symbol?',options:['=','+','#','@'],answer:0,right:'Correct. All formulas start with =.'},{q:'Which symbol means multiply?',options:['*','x','%','&'],answer:0,right:'Correct. * means multiply.'},{q:'What does =A2*B2 mean?',options:['Multiply the value in A2 by the value in B2','Add A2 and B2','Type the letters A2 B2','Multiply 2 by 2'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'Income = Quantity × Price. Quantity is in B2 and Price is in C2. Which formula is correct?',options:['=B2*C2','=B2+C2','=Quantity*Price','B2*C2'],answer:0,right:'Correct - and do not forget the = sign.'},{q:'You copy =B2*C2 from D2 down into D3. What does it become?',options:['=B3*C3','=B2*C2','=C2*D2','=B4*C4'],answer:0,right:'Correct. The references move down with the formula.'},{q:'Why is =A2*B2 better than typing =2*3 directly?',options:['If the numbers change, the answer updates automatically','It looks nicer','It is shorter to type','It uses less memory'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Cell references keep results up to date.'},{q:'Which formula divides the value in A5 by the value in A6?',options:['=A5/A6','=A5*A6','=A5\\A6','=A6/A5'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'}]);

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 in a row correctly. The step marks complete automatically.
steps.js:
(function(){var bank=[{q:'Quantity is in B2, Price is in C2. Formula for Income?',o:['=B2*C2','=B2+C2','=C2-B2','B2*C2'],a:0},{q:'Which symbol divides?',o:['/','*','-','+'],a:0},{q:'A formula always starts with...',o:['=','+','!','?'],a:0},{q:'Copy =D2*E2 down one row. It becomes...',o:['=D3*E3','=D2*E2','=E2*F2','=D1*E1'],a:0},{q:'Cost is in A4, VAT is in B4. Formula to add them?',o:['=A4+B4','=A4*B4','=A4-B4','A4+B4'],a:0},{q:'Which means multiply?',o:['*','x','/','+'],a:0},{q:'Total in C2 = Price A2 times Quantity B2. Formula?',o:['=A2*B2','=A2/B2','=A2+B2','=A2-B2'],a:0},{q:'Why use cell references in formulas?',o:['Results update when data changes','They are colourful','They are faster to delete','They hide the data'],a:0}];var solved=0,c=document.getElementById('l2-endless');function pick(){return bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)];}function render(){var q=pick();var opts=q.o.map(function(x,i){return{x:x,i:i};}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});c.innerHTML='

Correct in a row: '+solved+' / 5

'+q.q+'

'+opts.map(function(o){return '';}).join('')+'
';c.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(function(btn){btn.onclick=function(){var fb=c.querySelector('.ex-feedback');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.i)===q.a){solved++;fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent=solved>=5?'Practice complete. Well done!':'Correct. Next question loading.';if(solved>=5)__markStepComplete();else setTimeout(render,700);}else{solved=0;fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite - your streak resets. Try again.';setTimeout(render,1100);}};});}render();})();
